message:
feat: Add experimental --use-agent-commander option

This commit adds the `--use-agent-commander` option to both solve.mjs
and hive.mjs. When enabled, this experimental flag delegates tool
execution to the agent-commander library instead of using embedded
logic.

Changes:
- Add --use-agent-commander option to solve.config.lib.mjs (hidden)
- Add --use-agent-commander option to hive.config.lib.mjs (hidden)
- Create agent-commander.lib.mjs wrapper library
- Integrate agent-commander in solve.mjs tool execution flow
- Pass --use-agent-commander flag through hive.mjs to solve workers
- Add agent-commander as optional dependency in package.json
- Add experiment scripts for testing the integration

The option is:
- Hidden from --help (experimental)
- Disabled by default
- Fails gracefully when agent-commander is not installed

Fixes #1043

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

issue.title: Sync with features required by https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind

repository.name: agent-commander
repository.description: A JavaScript library to control agents enclosed in CLI commands like Anthropic Claude Code CLI

issue.body:
In order to fully support https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/pull/1044 we need to double check that https://github.com/link-assistant/agent-commander fully support all features required to support calls to codex, claude, agent, opencode from https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind codebase.

Please take full tree of src of https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind, read each file, and while you do read each file, also check that all necessary features are present in https://github.com/link-assistant/agent-commander.

After reading each file make sure to include in case study findings.

As the result we should find all missing features in agent-commander that are required in hive-mind.

Once you finish with this plan, you can immediately execute it in this pull request fully. Make sure we support both Rust and JavaScript of agent-commander with same features, and also have near 100% test coverage for all use cases from hive mind repository. Also make sure all our docs and code are consistent and in sync.

We need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

Please plan and execute everything in a single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context autocompacts and you can continue indefinetely, do as much as possible in one go, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.

body:
Get latest changes from default branch, note that codex support and claude support has changed significantly.

We need to carefully double check, that we don't break any existing code, and without `--use-agent-commander` everything will work as it was working before.

Yet with `--use-agent-commander` we should use latest version of https://github.com/link-assistant/agent-commander for all tools - claude, codex, agent, opencode, but only if this option selected. Also we need to make sure that when  `--use-agent-commander` we support each and every feature we have, but control tools (claude, codex, agent, opencode) only via our https://github.com/link-assistant/agent-commander, not directly. But only if `--use-agent-commander` is enabled.

Please also carefully list all the features we need in hive-mind, and we have in agent-commander, and if any feature is missing, make a code draft, but also report the issue to https://github.com/link-assistant/agent-commander, so I can pause this pull request, to fix all issues and add all missing features.

So we fully support `--use-agent-commander` when it is enabled.

We need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

Please plan and execute everything in a single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context autocompacts and you can continue indefinetely, do as much as possible in one go, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done. For issues use gh tool and create them directly on https://github.com/link-assistant/agent-commander if needed.

issue.title: Expose hive-mind execution parity options for claude/codex/opencode/agent

repository.name: agent-commander
repository.description: A JavaScript library to control agents enclosed in CLI commands like Anthropic Claude Code CLI

issue.body:
During hive-mind PR https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/pull/1044 for issue https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1043, I reviewed the latest JavaScript package `agent-commander@0.4.3` against hive-mind's embedded tool adapters.

The current hive-mind integration can route basic execution through `agent-commander`, but several hive-mind options still cannot be expressed through the public agent-commander API without falling back to direct tool-specific command construction.

Needed parity surface:

- Claude: thinking budget / effort env handling, 1M context disable behavior, sub-session auto-compact envs, MCP config without Playwright, useless/disallowed tool flags, read-only / permission modes, verbose/fallback/resume/session behavior, and custom binary/env passthrough.
- Codex: reasoning effort mapping, context window / auto-compact config, MCP config generation, sandbox/approval mode parity, custom binary/env passthrough, and the prompt-file path used for large prompts.
- OpenCode: config/permission env parity, custom binary/env passthrough, and prompt-file or equivalent large-prompt support.
- `agent`: hive-mind verbose handling, resume/no-fork equivalents where supported, permission/config parity, custom binary/env passthrough, and full JSON usage/result reporting.

Request:

Provide typed agent-commander options or a documented raw args/env/config passthrough that lets hive-mind control these features only through agent-commander when `--use-agent-commander` is enabled. This would let hive-mind remove remaining adapter-level compromises while keeping default embedded execution unchanged.

issue.title: Expose normalized result metadata for hive-mind solve sessions

repository.name: agent-commander
repository.description: A JavaScript library to control agents enclosed in CLI commands like Anthropic Claude Code CLI

issue.body:
During hive-mind PR https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/pull/1044 for issue https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1043, the experimental `--use-agent-commander` adapter had to best-effort parse raw/plain output from `agent-commander@0.4.3` to populate hive-mind's solve result fields.

Hive-mind currently consumes normalized metadata from its embedded adapters, including:

- success / non-zero exit classification
- session id
- usage-limit detection and reset time/timezone
- human-readable result summary
- Anthropic total cost where available
- public pricing estimate where available
- detailed model usage
- stream token usage
- sub-agent call summaries
- execution error classification

The current agent-commander result object has useful `exitCode`, `output.plain`, `output.parsed`, `sessionId`, and `usage` fields, but hive-mind still needs adapter-specific parsing to derive the complete normalized result shape.

Request:

Expose a stable normalized result metadata object from agent-commander for each supported tool (`claude`, `codex`, `opencode`, `agent`) so callers like hive-mind can avoid parsing tool-specific output while still reporting costs, usage limits, summaries, and failures consistently.

message: Merge main and finalize agent-commander option
